source_metadata.title_raw: Salvador V. Navarrete - I-18-271 - December 14, 2018 - Lathrop

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Salvador V. Navarrete 
City Attorney 
390 Towne Centre Drive 
Lathrop, CA 95330 
January 14, 2019 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-18-271 
Dear Mr. Navarrete: 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Lathrop City Councilwoman 
Diane Lazard regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). 1 
Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and 
not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or 
Section 1090. Because your request seeks general guidance, and does not pertain to any specific 
governmental decision, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance. 2 
Also note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71 ), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. 
QUESTION 
Can Councilwoman Lazard take part in City Council decisions involving River Islands 
Development, LLC (RID), given that one RID officer, Susan Dell'Osso, is also the co-owner of 
Dell'Osso Farms, LLC, where Councilwoman Lazard is employed? 
CONCLUSION 
In general, Councilwoman Lazard may not make, participate in making, or use her position 
to influence any City Council decisions with a foreseeable and material financial effect on RID 
including decisions related to RID's development of a master-planned community. Councilwoman 
Lazard has a financial interest in Susan and Ron Dell'Osso as a source of income and may not take 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal 
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).) 


File No. I-18-271 
Page No. 2 
part in decisions that may foreseeably and materially affect any of the Dell'Osso's interests, 
including RID. 
FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
Newly-elected Councilwoman Diane Lazard is an employee of Dell'Osso Farms, and will 
not participate in votes regarding that company. One of the co-owners of Dell'Osso Farms is Susan 
Del'Osso, who is also employed as an officer of RID. Councilwoman Lazard receives no 
compensation from RID, and the two companies are not related to each other. 
RID is developing a master-planned community of approximately 4,905 total acres within 
the City of Lathrop. The company plans to seek Lathrop City Council approval on various items 
over the next twenty-plus years. In response to a follow-up email I sent you, Susan Dell'Osso 
directly e-mailed me and stated that she owns 41 % ofDell'Osso Farms and her husband, Ron, owns 
58% of the company, for a total of 99% ownership. 
ANALYSIS 
Under Section 87100 of the Act, "[ n ]o public official at any level of state or local 
government shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to 
influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial 
interest." 
Financial Interest 
A public official has a financial interest under the Act if it is reasonably foreseeable that the 
decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, 
on the interests identified in Section 87103. As pertinent to the facts provided, potentially 
disqualifying interests include the following: 
• 
A public official's own personal finances, or those of a member of his or her immediate 
family. (Section 87103.) 
• 
Any business entity in which the public official has a direct or indirect investment worth two 
thousand dollars ($2,000) or more. (Section 87103(a).) 
• 
Any source of income aggregating five hundred dollars ($500) or more in value provided or 
promised to, or received by the public official within 12 months prior to the time when the 
decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) 
In addition to having an interest in any business entity from which the official has received 
income of $500 or mor~ within 12 months before the relevant governmental decision is made, the 
official has a source-of-income interest in any individual owning a 50 percent or greater interest in 
that business identity. (Regulation 18700.l(a)(2)(A).) The offic~al also has a source-of-income 
interest in any individual, regardless of the extent of the individual's ownership interest in the 
business entity, if that individual has the power to direct or cause the direction of the management 
and policies of the business entity. (Regulation 18700.l(a)(2)(B).) 


File No. 1-18-271 
Page No. 3 
Based on the information provided, Councilwoman Lazard has an interest in Dell'Osso 
Farms as a source of income under Section 87103(c).3 Additionally, she has interests in both Susan 
and Ron Dell'Osso as sources of income under Regulation 18700.l(a)(2)(A). We note that while 
Susan Dell'Osso does not individually hold a 50 percent interest in Dell'Osso Farms, her ownership 
interest for purposes of the Act includes her spouse's ownership interest. Accordingly, Susan 
Dell'Osso has a 99% ownership interest in Dell'Osso Farms for purposes of Regulation 
18700.1 (a)(2)(A). 
Foreseeability 
A financial effect on an interest is presumed to be reasonably foreseeable if the interest is a 
named party in, or the subject of, a governmental decision before the official or the official's 
agency. (Regulation 18701(a).) Regarding interests not explicitly involved in a decision, a financial 
effect need not be likely to be considered reasonably foreseeable. In general, if the financial effect 
can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical, it is reasonably 
foreseeable. If the financial result cannot be expected absent extraordinary circumstances not 
subject to the public official's control, it is not reasonably foreseeable. (Regulation 1870l(b).) 
Materiality 
A financial effect on a public official's interest in a business entity as a source of income is 
material if the business will be affected under the standards as applied to an interest in Regulation 
18702.1. (Regulation 18702.3(a)(4).) With respect to sources of income who are individuals not 
explicitly involved in a decision, Regulation l 8702.3(a)(2) provides that a decision's reasonably 
foreseeable financial effect on the interest is material if the individual "will be financially affected 
under the standards applied to an official in Regulation 18702.5, or the official knows or has reason 
to know that the individual has an interest in a business entity or real property that will be 
financially affected under the standards applied to a financial interest in Regulation 18702.1 or 
18702.2, respectively." 
Under Regulation 18702.1, a financial effect on an interest in a business entity is material in 
numerous circumstances, including whenever the business entity: 
• 
Initiates the proceeding in which the governmental decision will be made by filing an 
application, claim, appeal, or request for other government action concerning the business 
entity. (Regulation 18702.l(a)(l).) 
• 
Bids on or enters into a written contract with the official's agency. (Regulation 
18702.l(a)(3).) 
• 
Applies for a permit, license, grant, tax credit, exception, variance, or other entitlement that 
the official's agency is authorized to issue. (Regulation 18702.l(a)(5).) 
• 
Is the subject of any inspection, action, or proceeding subject to the regulatory authority of 
the official's agency. (Regulation 18702.l(a)(7).) 
3 This assumes that, at the time of each governmental decision, Councilwoman Lazard will have received at 
least $500 for her work for Dell'Osso Farms duririg the preceding twelve months. 


FileNo. 1-18-271 
Page No. 4 
A financial effect on an interest in a business entity is also material if a prudent person with 
sufficient information would find it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision's financial effect 
would contribute to a change in the price of the business entity's publicly traded stock, or the value 
of a privately-held business entity. (Regulation 18702.1 (b ). ) Examples of decisions that may be 
applicable include those that: 
• 
Authorize, prohibit, regulate, or otherwise establish conditions for an activity in which the 
business entity is engaged; 
• 
Increase or decrease the amount of competition in the field in which the business entity is 
engaged; 
• 
Increase or decrease the need for the products or services that the business entity supplies; 
• 
Make improvements in the surrounding neighborhood such as redevelopment projects, 
traffic/road improvements, or parking changes that may affect, temporarily or permanently, 
the amount of business the entity draws from the location of the project; or 
• 
Decide the location of a major development, entertainment facility, or other project that 
would increase or decrease the amount of business the entity draws from the location of the 
project; or 
• 
Increase or decrease the tax burden, debt, or financial or legal liability of the business entity. 
(Regulation 18702.l(b).) 
In short, if a public official has a source-of-income interest in an individual, and the official 
knows or has reason to know that the individual has an interest in a business entity that will be 
financially affected by a decision under the standards applied to an interest in Regulation 18702.1 
or 18702.2, the official is disqualified from taking part in the decision. In this case Councilwoman 
Lazard knows that Susan and Ron Dell'Osso have a 99% interest in Del'Osso farms and that Susan 
Dell'Osso has an interest in RID. Accordingly, Councilwoman Lazard is generally prohibited from 
taking part in decisions with a foreseeable and material financial effect on RID, 
More specifically, you stated that "RID is developing [a] master planned community of 
approximately 4,905 total acres within the City of Lathrop and RID plans to seek Council approval 
of various items over the next 20+ years." As the named party in, or the subject of, these future 
decisions, the financial effect of these decisions on RID is both foreseeable and material. Generally, 
Councilwoman Lazard will be prohibited from taking part in these decisions except in the limited 
circumstances when an exception to the Act's conflict of interest rules may apply. To the extent that 
Councilwoman Lazard needs additional help regarding any specific decision before the City, she 
should seek additional advice specifically identifying the decision in question. 


File No. I-18-271 
Page No. 5 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
KMC:jgl 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
By: 
Kevin Cornwall 
Counsel, Legal Division

written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)  
part in decisions that may foreseeably and materially affect any of the Dell'Osso's interests, 
including RID.

•

3 This assumes that, at the time of each governmental decision, Councilwoman Lazard will have received at 
least $500 for her work for Dell'Osso Farms duririg the preceding twelve months.

Development, LLC (RID), given that one RID officer, Susan Dell'Osso, is also the co-owner of 
Dell'Osso Farms, LLC, where Councilwoman Lazard is employed?

CONCLUSION: In general, Councilwoman Lazard may not make, participate in making, or use her position 
to influence any City Council decisions with a foreseeable and material financial effect on RID 
including decisions related to RID's development of a master-planned community. Councilwoman 
Lazard has a financial interest in Susan and Ron Dell'Osso as a source of income and may not take2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal 
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)  
part in decisions that may foreseeably and materially affect any of the Dell'Osso's interests, 
including RID.
